The most common herbal preparations made with Ginkgo biloba include capsules, tablets, and teas, which are often used to support cognitive function and memory.
taxonomy: Bilabrella robusta is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. Bilabrella robusta is a small, herbaceous perennial plant that grows in mountainous regions. The plant has a distinctive two-lipped corolla, hence its name Bilabrella, which translates to "two-lipped" in Latin. Bilabrella robusta is classified within the genus Bilabrella, which is part of the larger family Gentianaceae.
